channel.title: Comments for page "10. Mr. Mission"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "10. Mr. Mission"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the whole "noir detective" vibe and I can't help but read it in this voice.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Calling it. It's not the guy who's anomalous. It's his hat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the weakest in the series in its writing, but I just like the character in the context of being a little mister for the reasons moose said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like these two bits could use some thesaurus assistance to reduce the repetition of the noted words:

… my thoughts wandered. Almost thoughtlessly, …

… Shutting the watch and clenching the old watch in a fist …

Just doesn't sound as smooth to me with currently. Just me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is Mr. Mission's schtick that he is noir?
I accept that it can match the Little Misters theme, but it doesn't work for me. No vote.

item.title: I don't want to step on anyone's toes...
item.content:encoded:
On my way, another step closer towards salvation, towards my ultimate-and likely final-case. Closer and closer I got, one step closer and one step deeper.

Flipping through the magazine, some drivel about the economy that I grabbed in the airport, my thoughts wondered.

The 'my ultimate-and likely final-case' should have em dashes instead of hyphens. And I'm REASONABLY certain that that's supposed to be 'my thoughts wandered' instead of 'my thoughts wondered'.

I realise I could probably edit that myself, but I'm too scared to…

Otherwise, this is a pretty good piece of writing, with some very pretty prose in it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only Mr. Mission would have a story like this.

Every Little Mister has some paranormal quality, but if the story works without mentioning it, it doesn't need to be clarified.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe I missed something, but I don't see anything SCP-ish about Mr. Mission. I just get that he's a "man on a mission" archetype. Where's the creepy? What's supernatural about him? Heck, it seems like all the Misters could be qualified for the "Mr. Mission" title. What with the quest for Mr. Redd and all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…

Wait…

*scratches chin*

Hmmm…

Don't forget the tags - the last two stories don't have them…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everything is just more suave and explodey around Mr. Mission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think my favorite part of this is the fridge logic: the anomalous nature of this guy is that he's James Bond.

I fully expect that the more anomalous stuff involves probability alteration: once Mr. Mission is involved, you can be sure events will unfold like a spy novel.

Also, this is entertaining and well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, Dr. Wondertainment screwed with someone's mind to force him to dress, act and speak like a noir detective?
